description: baseline
Halve TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE to 2^18 for 2x more optimizer steps within the 5-minute budget, which typically improves final val_bpb by allowing more weight updates
Increase WARMDOWN_RATIO to 0.75 so more of the training budget is spent in LR cooldown phase, which helps the model converge to a better minimum
Add SwiGLU activation in MLP (replace ReLU^2 with SwiGLU gated linear unit) which has been shown to improve language model performance
Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine decay schedule, which provides a smoother transition and typically finds better minima
Combine two proven wins: halve TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE to 2^18 for more optimizer steps AND increase WARMDOWN_RATIO to 0.75 for better LR cooldown - neither was tried together
Increase weight decay on embedding, lm_head, and value_embeds from 0.001 to 0.003, as proven technique suggests this range often helps regularize large embedding tables
Increase WARMDOWN_RATIO to 0.75 (proven best standalone result) - current script has reverted to 0.5, restoring the best known setting
Replace adaptive cosine gradient clipping (1.0->0.3) with fixed clip norm of 1.0, allowing more aggressive gradient updates throughout training
Restore WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.75 (proven best standalone result of 0.9766) - current script reverted to 0.5, this is the single most reliable improvement found

Remove the internal residual connection in MLP.forward - the Block already adds the residual (x + self.mlp(norm(x))), so MLP having its own internal residual creates an unintended double-residual that
Increase MATRIX_LR from 0.04 to 0.05 to get more aggressive Muon updates for matrix parameters, potentially improving convergence speed within the 5-minute budget
